titleStmt.title: SII 30.116: original edition by N. Marxia Gandhi, A. Padmavathy and S. Swaminathan
No. 116. (A.R. No. 194 of 1915.) Brahmadēsam, Cheyyār Taluk, North Arcot District. Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 13, day 120: 972 A.D.

summary.p: This inscription records a gift of thirty kaḻañju of gold [tuḷai-niṟai-pōn] for two perpetual lamps to the temple of Śri Pōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ by Chaturan Muvāyiravaṉ, as Madhyasthan [Trairājyaghaṭikā Madhyasthan] and also an officer who belongs to the Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Tiruvēgampapuram in Dāmar-kōṭṭam.

This money has been utilized (as deposit) for making the temple land as tax-free and from the yield of that land, the mahāsabhā agreed to maintain the perpetual lamps. The Gaṇavāriyapperumakkaḷ undertook the responsibility of burning the two lamps.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . koppārtthiventripanmaṟku
yāṇṭu 10 3 100 100 2 10 tāmar koṭṭattu ti
ru vekampapuṟattu rājamallaca
turvveti maṅkalattu trairājyakhaṭikā
madhyasasdhan śrīadhikāriyākiya caturaṉ muvāyiravan Ivvūr śrī
pontaipperumāṉaṭikaḷukku Iraṇṭu nan=tāviḷakku Iravum
pakaluñ cantrādityavat Erippataṟku tan=tapoṉ dhanmakaṭṭaḷai
tuḷai niṟai muppatinkaḻañcu Ippoṉ muppatin kaḻañcum Ide
varkke kuṭuttu Idevaruṭaiya bhūmi tirucuṟṟālaiyiṉ teṟkil naṅ
kai ceyyāḷākiya Aṟunūṟu kuḻiyu muḷḷeRead- muṉṉeIṟaiyiliyāna
maiyil Icceṟuvil pon=ta bhogaṅkoṇṭu Iraṇṭu nantā
viḷakkum Erippatāka śilālekhai ceytu kuṭuttom mahā
sabhaiperumakkaḷom Itu Avvāṇṭu kaṇavāri
yañ ceyyum perumakkaḷey Eri(y)ppikka kaṭavārākavum
Eri(y)ppiyātu muṭṭil śratthāmandarey Avvavāṇṭu ceyyu
ṅkaṇavāriyap perumakkaḷaiye peṟuvakai nicatam Arai
kkāṟp poṉ Aṉṟāḻkovukku maṉṟap peṟuvārākavum I
pparicu śilālekhai ceyvi(y)ttuk kuṭuttom
mahāsabhaip perumakkaḷom perumakkaḷ paṇippa
Eḻutiṉeṉ trairājyaghaṭikā madhyasthan tiruvekampa kaṟpi
ccaṉeṉ I[p.*]paṭiy .
Digital edition of SII 30.116 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
